name: Zhong Fu
location: On the lateral aspect of the chest, in the first intercostal space, 6 cun lateral to the anterior midline, 1 cun below Yunmen LU-2
categories: Meeting point of LU and SP
indications: Pain in the shoulder and back
boardPearl: Front-Mu point of the Lung. Mu points are used to diagnose and treat disorders of their associated organ, especially in acute/excess conditions.
memoryTrick: Central Treasury = the Lung's front door (Mu point). The treasury stores the Qi of the Lung.
cautions: Do not puncture deeply — risk of pneumothorax

name: Yun Men
location: Below the lateral end of the clavicle, 6 cun lateral to the anterior midline, in the depression of the delto-pectoral triangle
indications: Pain in the shoulder and arm
boardPearl: Cloud Gate is above LU-1 and below the clavicle. The clouds (Qi) gather before descending into the Lung treasury (LU-1).
memoryTrick: Cloud Gate = clouds (Qi of respiration) enter through the gate below the clavicle. 1 cun above LU-1.
cautions: Do not puncture deeply — risk of pneumothorax

name: Tian Fu
location: On the medial aspect of the upper arm, 3 cun below the anterior axillary fold, on the radial side of the biceps brachii muscle
indications: Pain in the medial aspect of the upper arm
boardPearl: Window of Heaven point — these points treat disorders of the head, face, throat and sensory organs, and regulate the ascending/descending of Qi between head and body.
memoryTrick: Celestial Storehouse = Window of Heaven. The celestial (heaven) storehouse stores the Lung Qi high on the arm near the shoulder.

name: Xia Bai
location: On the medial aspect of the upper arm, 4 cun below the anterior axillary fold, 1 cun below LU-3, on the radial side of the biceps brachii muscle
indications: Pain in the medial aspect of the upper arm
boardPearl: Rarely tested. Located 1 cun below LU-3. The name references white, the color associated with Metal/Lung.
memoryTrick: Guarding White = white is the color of Metal (Lung). This point guards the white (Lung Qi) on the upper arm.

name: Chi Ze
location: At the cubital crease, in the depression lateral to the tendon of the biceps brachii muscle
depth: 0.5-1 cun perpendicularly, or prick to bleed
indications: Pain and spasm of the elbow and arm
boardPearl: He-Sea/Water point and Sedation point of the Lung channel. Water is the child of Metal — used to sedate excess in the Lung. He-Sea points treat diarrhea, skin diseases, and disorders of the fu organs. Excellent for clearing Lung heat.
memoryTrick: Cubit Marsh = a marsh (water) at the elbow crease (cubit). Water sedates Metal (mother-child). The marsh collects and drains excess.
cautions: Avoid the brachial artery medially

name: Kong Zui
location: On the palmar aspect of the forearm, on the line connecting LU-5 to LU-9, 7 cun above the transverse crease of the wrist
indications: Pain in the elbow and arm
boardPearl: Xi-Cleft point of the Lung channel. Xi-Cleft points treat acute conditions and pain of their associated channel and organ. For Yin channels, Xi-Cleft points especially treat blood conditions (e.g., hemoptysis for LU-6).
memoryTrick: Collection Hole = Xi-Cleft. Qi and Blood collect deeply at this hole. Think of it as the emergency point for acute Lung problems, especially coughing blood.

name: Lie Que
location: On the radial aspect of the forearm, superior to the styloid process of the radius, 1.5 cun above the transverse crease of the wrist
categories: Command point for Head and Nape
Ma Dan Yang Heavenly Star point
Exit point of the Lung channel
boardPearl: The single most important point on the Lung channel. Luo-Connecting point — connects LU to LI. Confluent point of the Ren Mai — paired with KI-6. Command point for the Head and Nape (Gao Wu). One of the Four Command Points. Exit point of the channel.
memoryTrick: Broken Sequence = the channel breaks away here to connect (Luo) to the LI channel. The sequence of the Lung channel is 'broken' as Qi diverges. Also the exit point — Qi leaves the LU channel here.

name: Jing Qu
location: On the radial aspect of the forearm, 1 cun above the transverse crease of the wrist, in the depression on the radial side of the radial artery
boardPearl: Jing-River/Metal point of the Lung channel. Since the Lung is a Metal organ, this is the Horary point (same element as the channel). Also functions as the Mother point on the Lung channel (Metal is the mother element of Metal in a Horary context). Jing-River points treat cough, asthma, and disorders of the voice.
memoryTrick: Channel Ditch = the channel runs through a ditch (depression) at the wrist. Metal on Metal = Horary point — use at Lung time (3-5 AM) for maximum effect.
cautions: Avoid the radial artery

name: Tai Yuan
location: At the transverse crease of the wrist, in the depression on the radial side of the radial artery, radial to the tendon of abductor pollicis longus
categories: Hui-Meeting point of Blood Vessels (8 Influential points)
boardPearl: Triple function point: Yuan-Source, Shu-Stream/Earth, and Hui-Meeting point of Blood Vessels (one of the 8 Influential/Hui points). Earth is the mother of Metal — therefore this is also the Tonification/Mother point. The pulse is classically taken at LU-9 (Cun position).
memoryTrick: Supreme Abyss = supremely deep and important. The 'supreme' reflects its status as the Influential point for all blood vessels. The abyss is the deep pulse-taking position at the wrist. Three roles: Yuan-Source, Tonification, and Influential for Vessels.
cautions: Avoid the radial artery

name: Yu Ji
location: On the thenar eminence, at the midpoint of the first metacarpal bone, on the border of the red and white skin (junction of the palmar and dorsal skin)
boardPearl: Ying-Spring/Fire point. Fire clears heat — use LU-10 to clear Lung heat and benefit the throat. Ying-Spring points clear heat from the channel. Combine with LU-5 (Water) for a powerful Lung heat-clearing combination.
memoryTrick: Fish Border = the thenar eminence looks like the belly of a fish, and the point is at the border between red and white skin (the fish's edge). Fire point = use to clear fire/heat from the Lung.

name: Shao Shang
location: On the radial side of the thumb, 0.1 cun from the corner of the nail
depth: 0.1 cun superficially, or prick to bleed
boardPearl: Jing-Well/Wood point. Jing-Well points clear heat, restore consciousness, and treat fullness below the Heart. Bled for acute sore throat and tonsillitis — one of the most important emergency uses on the Lung channel. The name 'Shang' refers to the Metal tone in Chinese music — 'Lesser Metal'.
memoryTrick: Lesser Shang = Lesser Metal (Shang is the musical note for Metal). The smallest (lesser) point on the channel at the tip of the thumb. Bleed this point for acute sore throat — think of releasing the heat from the very tip.

name: Shang Yang
location: On the radial side of the index finger, 0.1 cun from the corner of the nail
depth: 0.1 cun superficially, or prick to bleed
categories: Entry point of the Large Intestine channel
boardPearl: Jing-Well/Metal point and Entry point of the LI channel. Jing-Well points clear heat, restore consciousness, and treat fullness below the Heart. Metal is the native element of the LI channel — this is also the Horary point.
memoryTrick: Yang Shang = Yang Metal. The Yang counterpart to LU-11 (Shao Shang/Lesser Metal). Qi enters the LI channel here from LU-7.

name: Er Jian
location: On the radial side of the index finger, distal to the second metacarpophalangeal joint, at the junction of the red and white skin
boardPearl: Ying-Spring/Water point and Sedation point. Water is the child of Metal — used to sedate excess in the LI channel. Ying-Spring points clear heat from the channel.
memoryTrick: Second Space = the second point, in the space (depression) distal to the 2nd MCP joint. Water sedates Metal (child drains the mother).

name: San Jian
location: On the radial side of the index finger, in the depression proximal to the head of the second metacarpal bone, proximal to the second metacarpophalangeal joint
boardPearl: Shu-Stream/Wood point. Shu-Stream points on Yang channels treat heaviness of the body and pain in the joints. They also treat intermittent diseases.
memoryTrick: Third Space = the third point, in the space proximal to the 2nd MCP joint. Think 'San' = three.

name: He Gu
location: On the dorsum of the hand, between the 1st and 2nd metacarpal bones, at the midpoint of the 2nd metacarpal bone on its radial side. Alternatively, where the transverse crease of the thumb, when adducted, ends on the dorsum
categories: Command point for Face and Mouth
Ma Dan Yang Heavenly Star point
indications: Pain and swelling of the eye
boardPearl: The most commonly used and versatile point in acupuncture. Yuan-Source point of the LI channel. Command point for the Face and Mouth (Gao Wu). One of the Four Command Points. Strongly moves Qi and Blood, expels Wind, releases the exterior, and stops pain. CONTRAINDICATED IN PREGNANCY — promotes labor and can cause miscarriage.
memoryTrick: Joining Valley = the valley (depression) where the 1st and 2nd metacarpals join. 'He' means union/joining. The valley between the thumb and index finger. Also known as 'Tiger's Mouth' (Hu Kou). Four Gates = LI-4 + LR-3.
cautions: CONTRAINDICATED IN PREGNANCY — strongly moves Qi and Blood, may induce labor or miscarriage

name: Yang Xi
location: On the radial side of the wrist, in the center of the anatomical snuffbox, in the depression between the tendons of extensor pollicis longus and extensor pollicis brevis, when the thumb is tilted upward
indications: Redness and pain of the eye
boardPearl: Jing-River/Fire point. Fire is the grandchild of Metal in the generation cycle. Jing-River points treat cough, asthma, chills and fever, and diseases of the voice and joints.
memoryTrick: Yang Stream = a yang stream in the anatomical snuffbox (the 'valley' at the wrist). The stream of Yang Qi flows through this narrow passage.

name: Pian Li
location: On the radial aspect of the forearm, 3 cun above LI-5, on the line connecting LI-5 to LI-11
boardPearl: Luo-Connecting point of the LI channel — connects LI to LU. Luo points treat disorders of their paired (internally-externally related) channel. Also treats edema — the Luo vessel spreads fluid.
memoryTrick: Veering Passageway = the channel veers off here to connect (Luo) to the Lung channel. The passageway is the connection between LI and LU.

name: Wen Liu
location: On the radial aspect of the forearm, 5 cun above the wrist crease, on the line connecting LI-5 to LI-11
indications: Aching of the shoulder and arm
boardPearl: Xi-Cleft point of the LI channel. Xi-Cleft points treat acute conditions and pain of their associated channel and organ. For Yang channels, Xi-Cleft points are especially used for acute pain.
memoryTrick: Warm Dwelling = warmth dwells here. The Xi-Cleft (accumulation) point is where Qi and Blood accumulate deeply — the warm dwelling is where they gather.

name: Xia Lian
location: On the radial aspect of the forearm, 4 cun below LI-11, on the line connecting LI-5 to LI-11
boardPearl: Lower Ridge — the lower of two ridge points (LI-8 and LI-9). Treats abdominal pain and borborygmus.
memoryTrick: Lower Ridge = the lower of the two ridge points on the forearm (LI-8 is lower, LI-9 is upper). Located 4 cun below LI-11.

name: Shang Lian
location: On the radial aspect of the forearm, 3 cun below LI-11, on the line connecting LI-5 to LI-11
indications: Pain and numbness of the arm
boardPearl: Upper Ridge — the upper of two ridge points. Located 3 cun below LI-11. Rarely tested independently.
memoryTrick: Upper Ridge = the upper of the two ridge points (LI-9 is upper, LI-8 is lower). Located 3 cun below LI-11, closer to the elbow than LI-8.

name: Shou San Li
location: On the radial aspect of the forearm, 2 cun below LI-11, on the line connecting LI-5 to LI-11
indications: Pain and weakness of the arm
boardPearl: Arm Three Miles — the upper limb counterpart to ST-36 (Leg Three Miles/Zu San Li). Both are Yangming channel points that tonify Qi, though ST-36 is far more commonly used.
memoryTrick: Arm Three Miles = the arm version of Zu San Li (ST-36). 'Shou' means hand/arm, 'San Li' means three miles. Located 2 cun below the elbow crease at LI-11.

name: Qu Chi
location: At the lateral end of the transverse cubital crease, midpoint between LU-5 and the lateral epicondyle of the humerus, when the elbow is flexed
categories: Ma Dan Yang Heavenly Star point
indications: Pain and swelling of the elbow and arm
boardPearl: He-Sea/Earth point and Tonification point (Earth is the mother of Metal). One of the most versatile points — major point for clearing heat (especially Yangming heat), treating skin diseases, and lowering blood pressure. One of Sun Si-miao's 13 Ghost points (Gui Chen/Ghost Minister) for treating mania and psycho-emotional disorders.
memoryTrick: Pool at the Crook = a pool of Qi at the crook (bend) of the elbow. When the elbow is flexed, the crease forms a pool. Earth tonifies Metal (mother supports child).

name: Zhou Liao
location: On the lateral aspect of the upper arm, 1 cun above LI-11, on the lateral supracondylar ridge of the humerus
indications: Pain, numbness and contracture of the elbow and arm
boardPearl: Local point for elbow pain. Located 1 cun above LI-11 on the lateral supracondylar ridge. Rarely tested.
memoryTrick: Elbow Crevice = the crevice (depression) just above the elbow at the supracondylar ridge. Think 'Zhou' = elbow.

name: Shou Wu Li
location: On the lateral aspect of the upper arm, 3 cun above LI-11, on the line connecting LI-11 to LI-15
indications: Pain and contracture of the elbow and arm
boardPearl: Classically contraindicated for needling in some texts. Located 3 cun above LI-11. Rarely used or tested.
memoryTrick: Arm Five Miles = 'Wu Li' means five miles. Five cun from the wrist crease area. Located on the upper arm between elbow and shoulder.
cautions: Classically listed as contraindicated for moxibustion in some texts

name: Bi Nao
location: On the lateral aspect of the upper arm, on the line connecting LI-11 to LI-15, at the insertion of the deltoid muscle
indications: Pain in the shoulder and arm
boardPearl: Local point for shoulder and arm pain. Located at the deltoid insertion. The name literally means 'upper arm muscle.'
memoryTrick: Bi Nao = upper arm. 'Bi' = arm, 'Nao' = upper part. Located right at the deltoid insertion on the lateral arm.

name: Jian Yu
location: Anteroinferior to the acromion, in the depression between the clavicular and acromial heads of the deltoid muscle, when the arm is fully abducted
categories: Meeting point of LI and Yang Qiao Mai
indications: Pain and motor impairment of the shoulder and upper arm
boardPearl: Meeting point of LI and Yang Qiao Mai. Major local point for shoulder pain — one of the most commonly used points for frozen shoulder and shoulder disorders. Locate with the arm abducted to reveal the depression.
memoryTrick: Shoulder Bone = the point is at the shoulder bone (acromion). 'Jian' = shoulder, 'Yu' = bone/corner. Abduct the arm to find the two depressions at the deltoid.
specialCategories: Meeting of LI and Yang Qiao Mai

name: Ju Gu
location: In the depression between the acromial end of the clavicle and the spine of the scapula
categories: Meeting point of LI and Yang Qiao Mai
indications: Pain and motor impairment of the shoulder and arm
boardPearl: Meeting point of LI and Yang Qiao Mai (same as LI-15). Located at the junction of clavicle and scapular spine. The 'great bone' refers to the prominent bony structures at the top of the shoulder.
memoryTrick: Great Bone = the great (large) bone at the top of the shoulder where clavicle meets scapula. 'Ju' = great, 'Gu' = bone.
cautions: Do not puncture deeply — underlying lung apex
specialCategories: Meeting of LI and Yang Qiao Mai

name: Tian Ding
location: On the lateral side of the neck, 1 cun below LI-18, on the posterior border of the sternocleidomastoid muscle
boardPearl: Located on the neck, 1 cun below LI-18. Treats throat disorders. The celestial tripod supports the head (heaven) on the neck.
memoryTrick: Celestial Tripod = the neck is the tripod that supports the celestial (heavenly) head. 'Tian' = celestial/heaven, 'Ding' = tripod.
cautions: Avoid the carotid artery and external jugular vein

name: Fu Tu
location: On the lateral side of the neck, level with the tip of the Adam's apple (laryngeal prominence), between the sternal and clavicular heads of the sternocleidomastoid muscle
boardPearl: Window of Heaven point. Window of Heaven points regulate the ascending and descending of Qi between the head and body, and treat disorders of the head, face, throat and sense organs. Located at the level of the Adam's apple between the two heads of the SCM.
memoryTrick: Support the Prominence = supports (Fu) the Adam's apple (the prominence/Tu). The point is level with the laryngeal prominence. Window of Heaven = regulates Qi flow to the head.
cautions: Avoid the carotid artery and external jugular vein. Do not needle deeply.

name: Kou He Liao
location: Directly below the lateral border of the nostril, level with Renzhong GV-26, at the lateral border of the philtrum
boardPearl: Located below the nostril, level with GV-26. Used for nasal and facial disorders. Less commonly tested than LI-20.
memoryTrick: Mouth Grain Crevice = the crevice near the mouth where grains (food) enter. 'Kou' = mouth, 'He' = grain, 'Liao' = crevice.

name: Ying Xiang
location: In the nasolabial groove, at the level of the midpoint of the lateral border of the ala nasi
depth: 0.3-0.5 cun obliquely along the nasolabial groove, or perpendicularly
categories: Meeting point of LI and ST
Exit point of the Large Intestine channel
indications: Itching and swelling of the face
boardPearl: Meeting point of LI and ST channels — the LI channel ends here and the ST channel begins nearby. Exit point of the LI channel. The single most important point for nasal congestion, sinusitis, and loss of smell. The name 'Welcome Fragrance' implies that treating this point allows the patient to welcome fragrances (smells) again.
memoryTrick: Welcome Fragrance = once this point is needled, the patient can welcome (smell) fragrances again. Located right beside the nose. The LI channel crosses to the opposite side here before connecting with ST-1.

name: Cheng Qi
location: Directly below the pupil, between the eyeball and the infraorbital ridge.
depth: 0.3-0.7 cun perpendicular, pushing the eyeball upward; no manipulation or only slight lift-and-thrust.
categories: Meeting of ST, CV, and Yang Qiao Mai
indications: redness and pain of the eye
boardPearl: Meeting point of ST, CV, and Yang Qiao Mai. Important for all eye disorders. Located between the eyeball and the infraorbital ridge.
memoryTrick: Cheng Qi = 'Tear Container' — contains tears right below the eye, where tears collect.
cautions: Do not puncture deeply to avoid damaging the eyeball. No strong manipulation. Press after withdrawal to prevent hematoma.
specialCategories: Meeting point of ST, CV, Yang Qiao Mai

name: Si Bai
location: Below the pupil, in the depression at the infraorbital foramen, approximately 1 cun below ST-1.
depth: 0.2-0.3 cun perpendicular or oblique insertion. Avoid deep needling into the infraorbital foramen.
indications: redness and pain of the eye
boardPearl: Located at the infraorbital foramen — important landmark for trigeminal neuralgia (infraorbital nerve, V2).
memoryTrick: Si Bai = 'Four Whites' — the four whites of the eye are visible in all directions from this point.
cautions: Avoid deep perpendicular insertion into the infraorbital foramen.

name: Ju Liao
location: Directly below the pupil, level with the lower border of the ala nasi, lateral to the nasolabial groove.
categories: Meeting of ST and Yang Qiao Mai
indications: swelling of the lip and cheek
boardPearl: Meeting point of ST and Yang Qiao Mai. Key point for facial conditions, located level with the ala nasi.
memoryTrick: Ju Liao = 'Great Crevice' — a large depression beside the nose.
specialCategories: Meeting point of ST and Yang Qiao Mai

name: Di Cang
location: 0.4 cun lateral to the corner of the mouth, directly below the pupil.
depth: 0.3-0.5 cun oblique, usually directed toward ST-6 (Jia Che).
categories: Meeting of ST, LI, Yang Qiao Mai, and Ren Mai
boardPearl: Meeting point of ST, LI, Yang Qiao Mai, and Ren Mai. Classic point for facial paralysis — often needled through to ST-6.
memoryTrick: Di Cang = 'Earth Granary' — the mouth is the storehouse (granary) of Earth (Stomach), where food enters.
specialCategories: Meeting point of ST, LI, Yang Qiao Mai, Ren Mai

name: Da Ying
location: Anterior to the angle of the mandible, on the anterior border of the masseter muscle, in a groove-like depression where the facial artery pulsates.
depth: 0.3-0.5 cun oblique. Avoid the facial artery.
boardPearl: Located where the facial artery can be palpated anterior to the angle of the mandible.
memoryTrick: Da Ying = 'Great Welcome' — welcomes you at the great angle of the jaw.
cautions: Avoid the facial artery.

name: Jia Che
location: One finger-breadth anterior and superior to the angle of the mandible, in the depression that appears when the masseter muscle is clenched.
depth: 0.3-0.5 cun perpendicular, or directed toward ST-4 (through-needling).
categories: Ma Dan Yang Heavenly Star point
boardPearl: Ma Dan Yang Heavenly Star point. Located in the belly of the masseter — clench teeth to find the depression. Classic combination with ST-4 for facial paralysis.
memoryTrick: Jia Che = 'Jaw Bone' — right on the jaw, clench to find it.
specialCategories: Ma Dan Yang Heavenly Star point

name: Xia Guan
location: In the depression at the lower border of the zygomatic arch, anterior to the condyloid process of the mandible. Located with the mouth closed.
boardPearl: Meeting of ST and GB. Must locate with mouth closed — the condyloid process slides forward when the mouth opens, filling the depression. Key point for TMJ and ear disorders.
memoryTrick: Xia Guan = 'Below the Joint' — below the TMJ (temporomandibular joint).
cautions: Locate with the mouth closed.
specialCategories: Meeting point of ST and GB

name: Tou Wei
location: 0.5 cun within the anterior hairline, at the corner of the forehead, 4.5 cun lateral to the midline.
categories: Meeting of ST, GB, and Yang Wei Mai
boardPearl: Meeting of ST, GB, and Yang Wei Mai. Located at the corner of the forehead, 0.5 cun within the hairline. Important for headaches, especially frontal and lateral.
memoryTrick: Tou Wei = 'Head Corner' — at the corner of the head, where the hairline turns.
specialCategories: Meeting point of ST, GB, Yang Wei Mai

name: Ren Ying
location: Level with the tip of the Adam's apple (laryngeal prominence), on the anterior border of the sternocleidomastoid muscle (SCM), where the common carotid artery pulsates.
depth: 0.3-0.5 cun perpendicular. AVOID the carotid artery.
boardPearl: Window of Heaven point and Sea of Qi point. Located at the carotid pulse — extremely important to avoid the artery. Used for goiter, hypertension, and throat conditions.
memoryTrick: Ren Ying = 'Man's Welcome' — where you greet a man by checking his pulse at the neck (carotid).
cautions: CAUTION: Carotid artery. Do not puncture the artery. Avoid strong stimulation. Contraindicated for bilateral needling simultaneously by some sources.

name: Shui Tu
location: At the anterior border of the sternocleidomastoid muscle, midway between ST-9 and ST-11.
boardPearl: Midpoint between ST-9 and ST-11 on the anterior border of SCM. Used for throat conditions.
memoryTrick: Shui Tu = 'Water Prominence' — a prominence of water (swelling) at the throat, like goiter.
cautions: Caution with the carotid artery nearby.

name: Qi She
location: At the superior border of the sternal end of the clavicle, between the sternal and clavicular heads of the sternocleidomastoid muscle.
boardPearl: Located between the two heads of the SCM at the clavicle — the Qi resides (abode) here at the base of the neck.
memoryTrick: Qi She = 'Qi Abode' — where Qi dwells at the base of the throat.
cautions: Avoid deep insertion due to proximity to major vessels and the lung apex.

name: Que Pen
location: In the supraclavicular fossa, 4 cun lateral to the anterior midline, at the midpoint of the clavicle.
depth: 0.3-0.5 cun perpendicular. DO NOT needle deeply.
categories: Meeting of ST, LI, SI, TE, and GB
boardPearl: Meeting point of ST, LI, SI, TE, and GB — five Yang channels meet here. CAUTION: pneumothorax with deep needling. The supraclavicular fossa is the 'empty basin.'
memoryTrick: Que Pen = 'Empty Basin' — the supraclavicular fossa looks like an empty basin/bowl. Five Yang channels pour into this basin.
cautions: CAUTION: Risk of pneumothorax. Do NOT needle deeply. The lung apex lies beneath this point.
specialCategories: Meeting point of ST, LI, SI, TE, GB

name: Qi Hu
location: At the lower border of the clavicle, 4 cun lateral to the anterior midline (Conception Vessel).
boardPearl: Located at the lower border of the clavicle, 4 cun lateral to the midline. The 'door' through which Qi enters the chest.
memoryTrick: Qi Hu = 'Qi Door' — the doorway for Qi entering the chest from the throat.
cautions: Avoid deep needling — risk of pneumothorax.

name: Ku Fang
location: In the 1st intercostal space, 4 cun lateral to the anterior midline.
indications: fullness of the chest and hypochondrium
boardPearl: In the 1st intercostal space, 4 cun lateral to the midline — part of the chest ST line.
memoryTrick: Ku Fang = 'Storeroom' — the chest stores the vital organs like a storeroom.
cautions: Avoid deep needling — risk of pneumothorax.

name: Wu Yi
location: In the 2nd intercostal space, 4 cun lateral to the anterior midline.
boardPearl: In the 2nd intercostal space, 4 cun lateral to midline. Used for breast disorders.
memoryTrick: Wu Yi = 'Room Screen' — a screen within the room of the chest.
cautions: Avoid deep needling — risk of pneumothorax.

name: Ying Chuang
location: In the 3rd intercostal space, 4 cun lateral to the anterior midline.
boardPearl: In the 3rd intercostal space, 4 cun lateral — the 'window' to the breast region.
memoryTrick: Ying Chuang = 'Breast Window' — a window looking onto the breast.
cautions: Avoid deep needling — risk of pneumothorax.

name: Ru Zhong
location: At the center of the nipple, in the 4th intercostal space.
depth: CONTRAINDICATED — do NOT needle or apply moxa. Landmark point only.
indications: Used as a landmark for locating other points (e.g., ST-18, CV-17). NOT used clinically.
boardPearl: ABSOLUTELY CONTRAINDICATED for needling and moxa. Used ONLY as a landmark. The nipple is at the 4th intercostal space, 4 cun lateral to midline. This is extremely high-yield for boards.
memoryTrick: Ru Zhong = 'Breast Center' = center of the nipple = DO NOT NEEDLE. If you see this on the board, the answer is 'contraindicated.'
cautions: CONTRAINDICATED for needling and moxibustion. Landmark point ONLY.

name: Ru Gen
location: In the 5th intercostal space, 4 cun lateral to the anterior midline, directly below the nipple.
boardPearl: In the 5th intercostal space, directly below the nipple. 'Root of the breast' — important for lactation and breast disorders.
memoryTrick: Ru Gen = 'Breast Root' — at the root (base) of the breast, one space below the nipple (ST-17).
cautions: Avoid deep needling — risk of pneumothorax.

name: Bu Rong
location: 6 cun above the umbilicus, 2 cun lateral to the anterior midline.
boardPearl: First of the abdominal ST points at 2 cun lateral to midline. 6 cun above the umbilicus — the uppermost ST abdominal point.
memoryTrick: Bu Rong = 'Not Contained' — the stomach is so full its contents cannot be contained (vomiting).
cautions: On the left side, avoid deep needling over the stomach; on the right, over the liver.

name: Cheng Man
location: 5 cun above the umbilicus, 2 cun lateral to the anterior midline.
boardPearl: 5 cun above umbilicus, 2 cun lateral. The name 'Assuming Fullness' relates to epigastric fullness.
memoryTrick: Cheng Man = 'Assuming Fullness' — the stomach assumes (receives) fullness from food.

name: Liang Men
location: 4 cun above the umbilicus, 2 cun lateral to the anterior midline.
boardPearl: 4 cun above the umbilicus, 2 cun lateral — at the same level as CV-12 (Front-Mu of Stomach). Important for gastric pain.
memoryTrick: Liang Men = 'Beam Gate' — the gate at the beam (level of CV-12), a structural support for the middle burner.

name: Guan Men
location: 3 cun above the umbilicus, 2 cun lateral to the anterior midline.
boardPearl: 3 cun above the umbilicus, 2 cun lateral to midline.
memoryTrick: Guan Men = 'Pass Gate' — a gate that food must pass through in the digestive process.

name: Tai Yi
location: 2 cun above the umbilicus, 2 cun lateral to the anterior midline.
boardPearl: 2 cun above the umbilicus, 2 cun lateral. 'Supreme Unity' — relates to mental-emotional disturbances as well as digestive issues.
memoryTrick: Tai Yi = 'Supreme Unity' — the great oneness, used for when the spirit is disturbed (mania, irritability).

name: Hua Rou Men
location: 1 cun above the umbilicus, 2 cun lateral to the anterior midline.
boardPearl: 1 cun above the umbilicus, 2 cun lateral. 'Slippery Flesh Gate' — relates to abnormal flesh/masses and vomiting of slippery fluids.
memoryTrick: Hua Rou Men = 'Slippery Flesh Gate' — the gate through which slippery (pathological) flesh or fluids pass.

name: Tian Shu
location: 2 cun lateral to the center of the umbilicus.
boardPearl: FRONT-MU OF LARGE INTESTINE (NOT Stomach — Stomach's Front-Mu is CV-12). This is a very commonly tested distinction on boards. Meeting of ST and SP. The 'celestial pivot' between upper and lower body, at the umbilicus level.
memoryTrick: Tian Shu = 'Celestial Pivot' — the pivot between heaven (upper body) and earth (lower body) at the navel. Front-Mu of LI, NOT ST.
cautions: Contraindicated for deep needling in pregnancy.
specialCategories: Meeting point of ST and SP

name: Wai Ling
location: 1 cun below the umbilicus, 2 cun lateral to the anterior midline.
boardPearl: 1 cun below the umbilicus, 2 cun lateral to the midline. A lesser-used abdominal ST point.
memoryTrick: Wai Ling = 'Outer Mound' — an outer mound on the belly, lateral to the midline.

name: Da Ju
location: 2 cun below the umbilicus, 2 cun lateral to the anterior midline.
boardPearl: 2 cun below the umbilicus, 2 cun lateral. Treats lower jiao and urogenital conditions.
memoryTrick: Da Ju = 'The Great' — a great point for lower abdominal and urogenital issues.

name: Shui Dao
location: 3 cun below the umbilicus, 2 cun lateral to the anterior midline.
boardPearl: 3 cun below the umbilicus, 2 cun lateral. 'Waterway' — regulates the water passages, treats urinary and reproductive conditions.
memoryTrick: Shui Dao = 'Waterway' — the waterways of the lower jiao (urinary and reproductive tracts).

name: Gui Lai
location: 4 cun below the umbilicus, 2 cun lateral to the anterior midline.
boardPearl: 4 cun below the umbilicus, 2 cun lateral. 'Return' — returns the menses, returns prolapsed organs. Key point for gynecological and urogenital conditions.
memoryTrick: Gui Lai = 'Return' — makes things return: the menses, the uterus (prolapse), fertility.

name: Qi Chong
location: 5 cun below the umbilicus, 2 cun lateral to the anterior midline, superior to the inguinal groove.
depth: 0.5-1.0 cun perpendicular. Avoid the femoral artery.
categories: Meeting of ST and Chong Mai
indications: swelling and pain of the external genitalia
boardPearl: Sea of Nourishment (Water and Grain) point and meeting point of ST and Chong Mai. Where the Chong Mai originates from/connects with the Stomach channel. The Qi surges here from the lower abdomen.
memoryTrick: Qi Chong = 'Surging Qi' — Qi surges upward here at the meeting of ST and Chong Mai (the 'Surging Vessel').
cautions: Avoid the femoral artery.
specialCategories: Meeting point of ST and Chong Mai

name: Bi Guan
location: On the anterior thigh, on the line connecting the anterior superior iliac spine (ASIS) to the lateral border of the patella, at the level of the perineum, in the depression lateral to the sartorius muscle.
indications: Pain and numbness of the thigh
boardPearl: Located at the level of the gluteal fold/perineum on the ASIS-to-patella line. The 'gate' to the thigh.
memoryTrick: Bi Guan = 'Thigh Gate' — the gateway to the thigh, where the leg begins.

name: Fu Tu
location: On the anterior thigh, on the line from ASIS to the lateral border of the patella, 6 cun above the superior border of the patella.
indications: Pain and numbness of the thigh and knee
boardPearl: 6 cun above the superior border of the patella on the ASIS-patella line. Not to be confused with LI-18 (Fu Tu on the neck).
memoryTrick: Fu Tu = 'Crouching Rabbit' — the bulge of the quadriceps muscle looks like a crouching rabbit.

name: Yin Shi
location: 3 cun above the superior border of the patella, on the line from ASIS to the lateral border of the patella.
indications: Pain and numbness of the knee and thigh
boardPearl: 3 cun above the superior border of the patella on the ASIS-patella line.
memoryTrick: Yin Shi = 'Yin Market' — a marketplace (shi) of Yin, where coldness and dampness can accumulate in the knee region.

name: Liang Qiu
location: 2 cun above the superior lateral border of the patella, between the rectus femoris and vastus lateralis muscles.
boardPearl: Xi-Cleft point of the Stomach channel. Used for ACUTE Stomach conditions — acute gastric pain, gastric hemorrhage. Xi-Cleft points accumulate Qi and Blood and are used for acute conditions and pain, especially bleeding for Yin channel Xi-Clefts.
memoryTrick: Liang Qiu = 'Beam Hill' — a hill (mound) on the beam of the thigh. Xi-Cleft = acute conditions, so think 'acute stomach emergency on the hill.'

name: Du Bi
location: Below the patella, in the depression lateral to the patellar ligament (lateral eye of the knee). Located with the knee flexed.
depth: 0.5-1.0 cun oblique, directed slightly toward the center of the knee joint.
boardPearl: The 'lateral eye of the knee' — also called the lateral infrapatellar fossa. Located with knee flexed. The medial eye is called Xi Yan (extra point). Classic point for all knee conditions.
memoryTrick: Du Bi = 'Calf's Nose' — the two depressions below the kneecap look like a calf's nostrils; ST-35 is the lateral one.

name: Zu San Li
location: 3 cun below ST-35 (Du Bi), one finger-breadth (1 cun) lateral to the anterior crest of the tibia, on the tibialis anterior muscle.
categories: Ma Dan Yang Heavenly Star point
boardPearl: THE most important Stomach channel point and arguably the most commonly tested point on the NCCAOM. He-Sea (Earth) point, Sea of Nourishment (Water and Grain), Ma Dan Yang Heavenly Star, Ghost point, and Command point for the abdomen (according to the Four Command Points). Earth point of an Earth channel. Premier Qi tonification point. ST-36 + SP-6 is the classic combination for Qi and Blood tonification.
memoryTrick: Zu San Li = 'Leg Three Miles' — after needling, you can walk three more miles. 3 cun below the knee = 'three' miles. The #1 tonification point in the body.
specialCategories: Ma Dan Yang Heavenly Star point
Tonification point (Earth point of Earth channel)

name: Shang Ju Xu
location: 6 cun below ST-35 (Du Bi), one finger-breadth lateral to the anterior crest of the tibia.
categories: Lower He-Sea of the Large Intestine
boardPearl: LOWER HE-SEA OF THE LARGE INTESTINE — this is extremely high-yield. The Lower He-Sea points treat Fu (Yang) organ disorders. 'The diseases of the Fu organs should be treated at the Lower He-Sea points.' ST-37 treats Large Intestine conditions (diarrhea, dysentery, appendicitis).
memoryTrick: Shang Ju Xu = 'Upper Great Void' — the UPPER void treats the UPPER (relatively) Fu organ: Large Intestine. ST-37 = LI, ST-39 = SI.

name: Tiao Kou
location: 8 cun below ST-35 (Du Bi), one finger-breadth lateral to the anterior crest of the tibia, midway between ST-35 and ST-41.
boardPearl: EMPIRICAL POINT FOR FROZEN SHOULDER (adhesive capsulitis). This is a classic distal point — needle ST-38 while the patient moves the affected shoulder. Very commonly tested.
memoryTrick: Tiao Kou = 'Ribbon Opening' — picture a ribbon tied around a frozen shoulder being opened (released) by needling this point on the leg.

name: Xia Ju Xu
location: 9 cun below ST-35 (Du Bi), one finger-breadth lateral to the anterior crest of the tibia.
categories: Lower He-Sea of the Small Intestine
boardPearl: LOWER HE-SEA OF THE SMALL INTESTINE. The LOWER void treats the LOWER Fu organ: Small Intestine. Remember: ST-37 (Upper) = LI, ST-39 (Lower) = SI. Also treats breast disorders due to the ST channel traversing the breast.
memoryTrick: Xia Ju Xu = 'Lower Great Void' — the LOWER void for the LOWER Fu organ (Small Intestine). Upper (Shang) = LI, Lower (Xia) = SI.

name: Feng Long
location: 8 cun above the tip of the lateral malleolus, two finger-breadths lateral to the anterior crest of the tibia (approximately one finger-breadth lateral to ST-38).
categories: Ma Dan Yang Heavenly Star point
boardPearl: THE primary point for PHLEGM in the entire body — all types of phlegm (substantial and insubstantial). Luo-Connecting point of ST. The Stomach/Spleen (Earth) is the source of phlegm, so ST's Luo point resolves phlegm. Ma Dan Yang Heavenly Star point. Connects to the Spleen channel.
memoryTrick: Feng Long = 'Bountiful Bulge' — the bountiful bulge of phlegm. When you see 'phlegm' on boards, think ST-40. The calf muscle bulges bountifully here.
specialCategories: Ma Dan Yang Heavenly Star point

name: Jie Xi
location: At the ankle joint, in the depression at the midpoint of the transverse crease of the ankle, between the tendons of extensor hallucis longus and extensor digitorum longus, level with the tip of the lateral malleolus.
boardPearl: Jing-River point, Fire element. TONIFICATION point of the Stomach channel (Fire is the Mother of Earth in the generating cycle — mother tonifies the child). Used for ankle disorders and mental-emotional conditions.
memoryTrick: Jie Xi = 'Ravine Divide' — at the ravine (crease) of the ankle that divides the leg from the foot. Fire point = Tonification (Mother of Earth).

name: Chong Yang
location: On the dorsum of the foot, at the highest point of the instep, in the depression between the tendons of extensor hallucis longus and extensor digitorum longus, where the dorsalis pedis artery pulsates.
depth: 0.3-0.5 cun perpendicular. Avoid the dorsalis pedis artery.
boardPearl: Yuan-Source point of the Stomach channel and Exit point of the ST channel. Located where the dorsalis pedis artery pulses on top of the foot. Yuan-Source points of Yang channels are near the Shu-Stream points. Used to assess the state of Stomach Qi.
memoryTrick: Chong Yang = 'Surging Yang' — Yang surges at the pulse of the dorsalis pedis artery on top of the foot. The pulse here checks Stomach Qi (post-heaven Qi).
cautions: Avoid the dorsalis pedis artery.

name: Xian Gu
location: In the depression distal to the junction of the 2nd and 3rd metatarsal bones, on the dorsum of the foot.
boardPearl: Shu-Stream point, Wood element. On Yang channels, Shu-Stream points treat heaviness of the body and joint pain. Located distal to the 2nd-3rd metatarsal junction.
memoryTrick: Xian Gu = 'Sunken Valley' — a sunken depression (valley) between the metatarsal bones of the foot.

name: Nei Ting
location: On the dorsum of the foot, between the 2nd and 3rd toes, proximal to the web margin.
categories: Ma Dan Yang Heavenly Star point
boardPearl: Ying-Spring point, Water element. Ma Dan Yang Heavenly Star point. THE primary point for clearing STOMACH FIRE/HEAT. Ying-Spring points clear heat from the channel. Classic for toothache, mouth ulcers, and Stomach Fire conditions.
memoryTrick: Nei Ting = 'Inner Court' — in the inner court (between the toes), where Stomach Fire is judged and cleared. Water (element) puts out Fire (Stomach Heat).
specialCategories: Ma Dan Yang Heavenly Star point

name: Li Dui
location: On the lateral side of the 2nd toe, 0.1 cun proximal to the corner of the toenail.
depth: 0.1 cun superficial or prick to bleed.
indications: coldness of the leg and foot
boardPearl: Jing-Well point, Metal element. SEDATION point of the Stomach channel (Metal is the Child of Earth in the generating cycle — child sedates the mother). Ghost point of Sun Si-miao. Jing-Well points are used for emergencies, loss of consciousness, and mental restlessness. Also treats excessive dreaming and insomnia.
memoryTrick: Li Dui = 'Severe Mouth' — a severe mouth condition (toothache, swelling) is sedated at this Well point. Metal (child) sedates Earth (mother).

name: Yin Bai
location: On the medial side of the great toe, 0.1 cun from the corner of the nail
depth: 0.1 cun oblique or prick to bleed; moxa 3-5 cones
categories: Sun Si Miao Ghost point (Ghost Fortress)
boardPearl: SP-1 is the primary point for controlling uterine bleeding, especially with moxa
memoryTrick: Hidden White hides the bleeding — moxa SP-1 to stop uterine hemorrhage

name: Da Du
location: On the medial side of the great toe, distal to the 1st metatarsophalangeal joint, at the junction of the red and white skin
boardPearl: SP-2 is the Tonification point of Spleen — Fire is mother of Earth
memoryTrick: The Great Metropolis tonifies the Spleen — Fire (mother) feeds Earth (child)

name: Tai Bai
location: On the medial side of the foot, proximal to the head of the 1st metatarsal bone, at the junction of the red and white skin
boardPearl: SP-3 is both Shu-Stream (Earth) and Yuan-Source — Shu-Stream and Yuan-Source coincide on Yin channels
memoryTrick: Supreme White = Supreme source of Earth energy — Shu-Stream and Yuan overlap on Yin channels

name: Gong Sun
location: On the medial side of the foot, in the depression distal and inferior to the base of the 1st metatarsal bone
boardPearl: SP-4 is the Confluent point of Chong Mai, paired with PC-6 for heart, chest, and stomach disorders
memoryTrick: Grandfather (SP-4) and Grandson (PC-6) treat Chong Mai — heart, chest, stomach

name: Shang Qiu
location: In the depression distal and inferior to the medial malleolus, midway between the tuberosity of the navicular bone and the tip of the medial malleolus
boardPearl: SP-5 is the Sedation point of Spleen — Metal is child of Earth
memoryTrick: Shang Mound sedates the Spleen — Metal (child) drains Earth (mother)

name: San Yin Jiao
location: 3 cun above the tip of the medial malleolus, on the posterior border of the tibia
categories: Meeting point of three Yin channels (SP, LR, KI)
boardPearl: SP-6 is the meeting point of three Yin channels of the leg (SP, LR, KI) — CONTRAINDICATED IN PREGNANCY
memoryTrick: Three Yin meet at the Intersection — 3 cun up, 3 channels, absolutely no pregnancy needling
cautions: CONTRAINDICATED IN PREGNANCY — can induce labor. Avoid strong stimulation in pregnant women.
specialCategories: Meeting point of SP, LR, KI

name: Lou Gu
location: 6 cun above the tip of the medial malleolus, on the posterior border of the tibia, 3 cun above SP-6
boardPearl: SP-7 is midway between medial malleolus and SP-9, 3 cun above SP-6
memoryTrick: Leaking Valley — 6 cun up from the valley of the malleolus

name: Di Ji
location: 3 cun below SP-9, on the line connecting SP-9 and the medial malleolus, on the posterior border of the tibia
boardPearl: SP-8 Xi-Cleft is the go-to point for acute dysmenorrhea and menstrual pain
memoryTrick: Earth's Crux — the critical emergency point for acute menstrual cramps

name: Yin Ling Quan
location: In the depression below the medial condyle of the tibia, at the junction of the shaft and the medial condyle of the tibia, level with the tibial tuberosity
boardPearl: SP-9 is THE point for resolving dampness — He-Sea Water point of the Spleen
memoryTrick: Yin Mound Spring drains the damp mound — #1 point for dampness anywhere in the body

name: Xue Hai
location: 2 cun above the mediosuperior border of the patella, in the bulge of the vastus medialis muscle
boardPearl: SP-10 Sea of Blood is the primary point for Blood disorders and skin conditions caused by Blood Heat
memoryTrick: Sea of Blood — a sea of relief for all blood and skin disorders

name: Ji Men
location: 6 cun above SP-10, on the line drawn from SP-10 to SP-12, over the sartorius muscle
boardPearl: SP-11 is located over the sartorius — caution femoral artery nearby
memoryTrick: Winnower Gate winnows along the inner thigh — watch for the artery
cautions: Avoid femoral artery

name: Chong Men
location: 3.5 cun lateral to CV-2 (on the upper border of the pubic symphysis), at the lateral end of the inguinal groove, lateral to the femoral artery
boardPearl: SP-12 is at the inguinal groove, lateral to the femoral artery — meeting of SP and LR
memoryTrick: The Surging Gate surges at the groin where SP meets LR
cautions: Avoid femoral artery

name: Fu She
location: 0.7 cun above SP-12, 4 cun lateral to the anterior midline
categories: Meeting of SP, LR, and Yin Wei Mai
boardPearl: SP-13 is a meeting point of SP, LR, and Yin Wei Mai
memoryTrick: Bowel Abode houses the bowels — where SP, LR, and Yin Wei meet
specialCategories: Meeting of SP, LR, Yin Wei Mai

name: Fu Jie
location: 1.3 cun below SP-15, 4 cun lateral to the anterior midline
boardPearl: SP-14 is 1.3 cun below SP-15 — located 4 cun lateral to midline
memoryTrick: The Abdomen Knot ties together just below Da Heng

name: Da Heng
location: 4 cun lateral to the center of the umbilicus
categories: Meeting of SP and Yin Wei Mai
boardPearl: SP-15 is 4 cun lateral to the umbilicus — important for treating constipation and diarrhea
memoryTrick: Great Horizontal lies horizontally level with the navel — 4 cun out
specialCategories: Meeting of SP and Yin Wei Mai

name: Fu Ai
location: 3 cun above SP-15, 4 cun lateral to the anterior midline
depth: 1-1.5 cun perpendicular; do not puncture deeply
categories: Meeting of SP and Yin Wei Mai
boardPearl: SP-16 — do not puncture deeply as the liver and spleen organs lie beneath
memoryTrick: Abdomen Sorrow — deep needling here causes real sorrow (organs beneath!)
cautions: Do not puncture deeply — liver and spleen organs lie beneath
specialCategories: Meeting of SP and Yin Wei Mai

name: Shi Dou
location: In the 5th intercostal space, 6 cun lateral to the anterior midline
depth: 0.5-0.8 cun oblique or transverse; do not puncture deeply
boardPearl: SP-17 through SP-20 are in the intercostal spaces, 6 cun lateral to midline
memoryTrick: Food Hole in the 5th ICS — the chest points count down from 5th to 2nd
cautions: Do not puncture deeply — risk of pneumothorax

name: Tian Xi
location: In the 4th intercostal space, 6 cun lateral to the anterior midline
depth: 0.5-0.8 cun oblique or transverse; do not puncture deeply
boardPearl: SP-18 in 4th ICS — used for breast and lactation problems
memoryTrick: Celestial Ravine in the 4th ICS — celestial milk for lactation
cautions: Do not puncture deeply — risk of pneumothorax

name: Xiong Xiang
location: In the 3rd intercostal space, 6 cun lateral to the anterior midline
depth: 0.5-0.8 cun oblique or transverse; do not puncture deeply
boardPearl: SP-19 in 3rd ICS, 6 cun lateral to midline
memoryTrick: Chest Village — a village in the 3rd ICS
cautions: Do not puncture deeply — risk of pneumothorax

name: Zhou Rong
location: In the 2nd intercostal space, 6 cun lateral to the anterior midline
depth: 0.5-0.8 cun oblique or transverse; do not puncture deeply
boardPearl: SP-20 in 2nd ICS, 6 cun lateral to midline
memoryTrick: All-Round Flourishing in the 2nd ICS — flourishing near the top of the chest
cautions: Do not puncture deeply — risk of pneumothorax

name: Da Bao
location: On the mid-axillary line, in the 6th intercostal space
depth: 0.5-0.8 cun oblique or transverse; do not puncture deeply
boardPearl: SP-21 is the Great Luo of the Spleen — it controls all the Luo-connecting vessels of the entire body
memoryTrick: Great Embracement embraces ALL the Luo vessels — the great network master
cautions: Do not puncture deeply — risk of pneumothorax

name: Ji Quan
location: In the center of the axilla, medial to the axillary artery
boardPearl: HT-1 is the entry point of the Heart channel, located in the center of the axilla
memoryTrick: The Highest Spring of the Heart bubbles up from the armpit — the highest point on the channel
cautions: Avoid axillary artery

name: Qing Ling
location: 3 cun above the medial epicondyle of the humerus, in the groove medial to the biceps brachii muscle
boardPearl: HT-2 is 3 cun above the medial epicondyle — infrequently tested
memoryTrick: Cyan Spirit — a blue-green spirit 3 cun above the elbow

name: Shao Hai
location: At the medial end of the cubital crease, in the depression anterior to the medial epicondyle of the humerus, with the elbow flexed
boardPearl: HT-3 He-Sea Water point — flex the elbow to locate at the medial end of the cubital crease
memoryTrick: Lesser Sea at the elbow crease — the Water He-Sea of the Heart

name: Ling Dao
location: 1.5 cun above the wrist crease, on the radial side of the flexor carpi ulnaris tendon
boardPearl: HT-4 is the Sedation point of Heart — Metal is child of Fire
memoryTrick: Spirit Pathway sedates — Metal (child) pathway drains Fire (mother)

name: Tong Li
location: 1 cun above the wrist crease, on the radial side of the flexor carpi ulnaris tendon
boardPearl: HT-5 Luo-Connecting benefits speech because the Heart opens to the tongue
memoryTrick: Tong Li connects to the tongue — the Heart's Luo makes you talk (Li = regulate)

name: Yin Xi
location: 0.5 cun above the wrist crease, on the radial side of the flexor carpi ulnaris tendon
boardPearl: HT-6 Xi-Cleft is the key point for night sweats (Yin deficiency with heat) and acute heart pain
memoryTrick: Yin Cleft clefts the night sweats — Xi-Cleft of the Yin Heart stops sweating at night

name: Shen Men
location: At the wrist crease, on the radial side of the flexor carpi ulnaris tendon, in the depression at the proximal border of the pisiform bone
boardPearl: HT-7 Shen Men is THE point for calming the Shen — insomnia, anxiety, palpitations, all mental/emotional disorders
memoryTrick: Spirit Gate opens the gate to peaceful spirit — THE Shen calmer, Yuan-Source + Shu-Stream at the wrist

name: Shao Fu
location: On the palm, between the 4th and 5th metacarpal bones, where the tip of the little finger rests when a fist is made
boardPearl: HT-8 is the Ying-Spring Fire point — Fire point on the Fire channel (horary point)
memoryTrick: Lesser Mansion where the little finger fist lands — Fire on Fire for the Heart's own element

name: Shao Chong
location: On the radial side of the little finger, 0.1 cun from the corner of the nail
depth: 0.1 cun shallow or prick to bleed
boardPearl: HT-9 is the Tonification point (Wood is mother of Fire) and Jing-Well — used for revival and loss of consciousness
memoryTrick: Lesser Surge surges life back — Wood (mother) tonifies Fire (child), Well point for revival

name: Shao Ze
location: On the ulnar side of the little finger, 0.1 cun from the corner of the nail
depth: 0.1 cun shallow or prick to bleed
boardPearl: SI-1 is classically used to promote lactation — bleed or moxa. Jing-Well Metal point.
memoryTrick: Lesser Marsh marshals the milk — bleed or moxa SI-1 for insufficient lactation

name: Qian Gu
location: Distal to the 5th metacarpophalangeal joint, on the ulnar side, at the junction of the red and white skin
boardPearl: SI-2 is the Sedation point of SI — Water is child of Metal (for Yang channel five-shu sequence)
memoryTrick: Front Valley sedates — Water drains from the front of the 5th MCP

name: Hou Xi
location: Proximal to the 5th metacarpophalangeal joint, on the ulnar side, at the end of the transverse crease, at the junction of the red and white skin, when a loose fist is made
categories: Ma Dan Yang Heavenly Star point
boardPearl: SI-3 is the Confluent point of Du Mai, paired with BL-62 — treats the entire spine, back, occiput, and inner canthus. Also a Ma Dan Yang Heavenly Star point.
memoryTrick: Back Ravine opens the Du (back) — the most important SI point, paired with BL-62 for the spine

name: Wan Gu
location: On the ulnar side of the wrist, in the depression between the base of the 5th metacarpal bone and the hamate and triquetral bones
boardPearl: SI-4 is the Yuan-Source point — on Yang channels, Yuan-Source is separate from Shu-Stream (unlike Yin channels)
memoryTrick: Wrist Bone = Yuan-Source at the wrist — Yang channels separate their Yuan from Shu

name: Yang Gu
location: On the ulnar side of the wrist, in the depression between the styloid process of the ulna and the triquetral bone
indications: Swelling of neck and submandibular region
boardPearl: SI-5 is the Jing-River Fire point — Fire point on the Fire channel (horary point of SI)
memoryTrick: Yang Valley is the Fire in the Fire channel — the horary point of SI

name: Yang Lao
location: On the dorsal aspect of the head of the ulna; with the palm facing the chest, in the bony cleft on the radial side of the styloid process of the ulna
indications: Pain in the shoulder, back, elbow, and arm
boardPearl: SI-6 'Nursing the Aged' — Xi-Cleft that benefits the eyes; commonly used for elderly patients with eye and neck problems
memoryTrick: Nursing the Aged helps grandpa see and turn his neck — Xi-Cleft for acute SI pain and elderly eye problems

name: Zhi Zheng
location: 5 cun above the wrist crease, on the line connecting SI-5 and SI-8
boardPearl: SI-7 Luo-Connecting is 5 cun above the wrist — connects to the paired Heart channel. Classically used for warts.
memoryTrick: Branch of the Correct branches off to the Heart at 5 cun — also treats warts

name: Xiao Hai
location: Between the tip of the olecranon and the medial epicondyle of the humerus, in the groove for the ulnar nerve
indications: Pain in the elbow and arm
boardPearl: SI-8 He-Sea Earth point is also the Tonification point (Earth is mother of Metal on Yang channels). Located in the ulnar nerve groove — the 'funny bone'.
memoryTrick: Small Sea at the funny bone — Earth tonifies this small sea where the ulnar nerve hides
cautions: Located in ulnar nerve groove — patient may experience electric sensation

name: Jian Zhen
location: Posterior and inferior to the shoulder joint, 1 cun above the posterior axillary fold, when the arm is adducted
indications: Pain in the shoulder and scapular region
boardPearl: SI-9 is 1 cun above the posterior axillary fold — the start of the scapular region points
memoryTrick: True Shoulder — the true start of the shoulder/scapula region, 1 cun above posterior axillary fold

name: Nao Shu
location: Directly above SI-9, in the depression inferior to the spine of the scapula, when the arm is adducted
categories: Meeting of SI, BL, Yang Qiao Mai, Yang Wei Mai
indications: Pain and weakness of shoulder and arm
boardPearl: SI-10 is directly above SI-9, below the spine of the scapula — meeting of SI, BL, Yang Qiao Mai, Yang Wei Mai
memoryTrick: Upper Arm Shu sits just below the scapular spine — where four pathways meet
specialCategories: Meeting of SI, BL, Yang Qiao Mai, Yang Wei Mai

name: Tian Zong
location: In the center of the infrascapular fossa, at the junction of the upper and middle third of the distance between the lower border of the scapular spine and the inferior angle of the scapula, level with the 4th thoracic vertebra
indications: Pain in lateral aspect of upper arm
boardPearl: SI-11 is an important local point for shoulder and scapular pain; also used for lactation and breast problems like SI-1
memoryTrick: Celestial Gathering gathers healing for the scapula and breast — center of the infrascapular fossa

name: Bing Feng
location: In the center of the suprascapular fossa, directly above SI-11, in the depression formed when the arm is raised
categories: Meeting of SI, LI, TE, GB
indications: Numbness and pain of upper extremity
boardPearl: SI-12 is directly above SI-11 in the suprascapular fossa — meeting of SI, LI, TE, GB
memoryTrick: Grasping the Wind above the scapular spine — four Yang channels meet to grasp Wind
specialCategories: Meeting of SI, LI, TE, GB

name: Qu Yuan
location: On the medial end of the suprascapular fossa, midway between SI-10 and the spinous process of T2
indications: Pain and stiffness of scapular region
boardPearl: SI-13 is at the medial end of the suprascapular fossa, midway between SI-10 and T2 spinous process
memoryTrick: Crooked Wall at the crooked medial corner of the suprascapular fossa

name: Jian Wai Shu
location: 3 cun lateral to the lower border of the spinous process of T1 (GV-13)
depth: 0.5-0.8 cun oblique; do not puncture deeply
indications: Pain and stiffness of shoulder and back
boardPearl: SI-14 is 3 cun lateral to T1 spinous process — outer to the Bladder channel
memoryTrick: Outer Shoulder Shu — 3 cun lateral to T1, outer to the BL lines
cautions: Do not puncture deeply — risk of pneumothorax

name: Jian Zhong Shu
location: 2 cun lateral to the lower border of the spinous process of C7 (GV-14)
depth: 0.5-0.8 cun oblique; do not puncture deeply
boardPearl: SI-15 is 2 cun lateral to GV-14 (C7) — the middle between the inner and outer shoulder Shu points
memoryTrick: Middle Shoulder Shu — 2 cun lateral to C7, the middle ground of the shoulder region
cautions: Do not puncture deeply — risk of pneumothorax

name: Tian Chuang
location: On the posterior border of the sternocleidomastoid muscle (SCM), level with the Adam's apple (laryngeal prominence), posterior to LI-18
boardPearl: SI-16 is a Window of Heaven point — located on the posterior border of SCM, level with the Adam's apple
memoryTrick: Celestial Window opens a window to heaven on the posterior SCM — lets Qi flow to the head
cautions: Avoid carotid artery and external jugular vein

name: Tian Rong
location: Posterior to the angle of the mandible, in the depression on the anterior border of the sternocleidomastoid muscle
boardPearl: SI-17 is a Window of Heaven point — posterior to the angle of the mandible, anterior border of SCM
memoryTrick: Celestial Appearance at the jaw angle — another Window of Heaven for the throat and ears
cautions: Avoid external carotid artery

name: Quan Liao
location: Directly below the outer canthus of the eye, in the depression below the zygomatic bone
boardPearl: SI-18 is directly below the outer canthus, below the zygomatic bone — meeting of SI and TE. Important for facial paralysis.
memoryTrick: Cheekbone Crevice hides in the crevice below the cheekbone — where SI meets TE on the face

name: Ting Gong
location: Between the tragus of the ear and the temporomandibular joint (TMJ), in the depression formed when the mouth is open
boardPearl: SI-19 is THE key point for ear disorders — deafness, tinnitus. Located between tragus and TMJ with mouth open. Meeting of SI, TE, GB.
memoryTrick: Auditory Palace is the palace of hearing — open your mouth to find it, the king of ear points

name: Yong Quan
location: On the sole of the foot, in a depression formed when the foot is plantar-flexed, approximately at the junction of the anterior and middle third of the sole, between the second and third metatarsal bones
boardPearl: Jing-Well/Wood point and Sedation point of the Kidney channel. Wood is the child of Water — used to sedate excess. The lowest point on the entire body. Major point for emergency revival and loss of consciousness. Descends excess from the head (vertex headache, hypertension). Clears Heat from Kidney Yin deficiency.
memoryTrick: Gushing Spring = water gushes up from the earth at the sole (the lowest spring on the body). As a Well point, this is where channel Qi emerges. The spring gushes upward to revive consciousness.
cautions: Moxibustion is generally contraindicated (some classical sources); needling can be very painful — the sole is sensitive

name: Ran Gu
location: On the medial aspect of the foot, below the navicular tuberosity, at the junction of the red and white skin
boardPearl: Ying-Spring/Fire point of the Kidney channel. Fire within Water — clears deficiency Heat, especially from Kidney Yin deficiency. Important for clearing empty Heat signs: night sweats, five-palm heat, tidal fevers. The name 'Blazing Valley' reflects Fire within the Water channel.
memoryTrick: Blazing Valley = Fire (Ying-Spring) blazing in the valley of the Water channel. Think: the Fire point on the Water channel — perfect for clearing deficiency heat from Kidney Yin xu.

name: Tai Xi
location: In the depression between the medial malleolus and the Achilles tendon, level with the prominence of the medial malleolus
boardPearl: Shu-Stream/Earth point and Yuan-Source point — the single most important point for tonifying the Kidney in all its aspects (Yin, Yang, Qi, Jing). Earth is the mother of Metal but here the key role is as Source point. Treats virtually any Kidney pattern. One of the most clinically significant points in all of acupuncture.
memoryTrick: Supreme Stream = the supreme (greatest) stream of Kidney Qi. As both Shu-Stream and Yuan-Source, this is THE go-to Kidney point. Located between the medial malleolus and Achilles — the supreme source of Kidney power.
cautions: Avoid the posterior tibial artery

name: Da Zhong
location: Posterior and inferior to the medial malleolus, in the depression anterior to the medial side of the attachment of the Achilles tendon
indications: Stiffness and pain of the lower back
boardPearl: Luo-Connecting point of the Kidney channel — connects KD to UB. Important for treating fear (the emotion of the Kidney) and strengthening the will (Zhi). Treats heel pain due to its location. The Luo vessel of the Kidney connects to the Pericardium and low back.
memoryTrick: Large Goblet = the Luo-Connecting point is like a large goblet that collects and distributes Qi between KD and UB. Located near the heel — think of a goblet sitting on the heel shelf.

name: Shui Quan
location: 1 cun directly below KD-3 Tai Xi, in the depression anterior and superior to the medial side of the calcaneus
boardPearl: Xi-Cleft point of the Kidney channel. Xi-Cleft points treat acute conditions — for Yin channels, especially blood conditions. Key for acute menstrual disorders (amenorrhea, dysmenorrhea). The name Water Spring reflects its role in regulating Water metabolism of the Kidney.
memoryTrick: Water Spring = Xi-Cleft of the Water channel. The spring is where water collects deeply (Xi = cleft/accumulation). Think acute Kidney blood issues — especially menstrual problems.

name: Zhao Hai
location: 1 cun below the tip of the medial malleolus, in the depression formed by two ligamentous bundles
categories: Confluent point of Yin Qiao Mai
boardPearl: Confluent point of the Yin Qiao Mai — paired with LU-7 (Ren Mai). Together they treat throat, chest, and Lung disorders. Benefits the throat (sore/dry throat is a hallmark indication). Nourishes Kidney Yin. Promotes sleep (Yin Qiao Mai controls closing of the eyes). Regulates the uterus.
memoryTrick: Shining Sea = Yin Qiao's sea of Yin shines like a calm, reflective ocean — promoting rest and sleep. Paired with LU-7 for the throat. Remember: KD-6 opens Yin Qiao, which governs sleep and closing of the eyes.

name: Fu Liu
location: 2 cun directly above KD-3 Tai Xi, on the anterior border of the Achilles tendon
boardPearl: Jing-River/Metal point and Tonification point of the Kidney channel. Metal is the mother of Water — tonifies via the mother-child rule. Key point for regulating sweating: resolves both spontaneous sweating (Qi xu) and night sweating (Yin xu). Resolves edema and regulates the Water passages.
memoryTrick: Recover Flow = recover the flow of Water (Kidney Qi) using Metal (the mother). As the tonification point, it recovers what has been lost. Classically the #1 point for sweating disorders — think of recovering the flow of fluids.

name: Jiao Xin
location: 2 cun above KD-3 Tai Xi, 0.5 cun anterior to KD-7 Fu Liu, posterior to the medial border of the tibia
boardPearl: Xi-Cleft point of the Yin Qiao Mai (NOT the Kidney channel Xi-Cleft — that is KD-5). Important for acute disorders of the Yin Qiao vessel. Treats menstrual and uterine disorders. Remember: KD-5 = Xi-Cleft of KD channel; KD-8 = Xi-Cleft of Yin Qiao Mai.
memoryTrick: Intersection Reach = the intersection (Xi-Cleft) of the Yin Qiao Mai, which reaches up from the ankle. Don't confuse with KD-5 (Xi-Cleft of the KD channel itself). KD-8 is the emergency point for Yin Qiao problems.

name: Zhu Bin
location: 5 cun above KD-3 Tai Xi, on the line connecting KD-3 and KD-10, about 1 cun posterior to the medial border of the tibia, on the medial head of the gastrocnemius muscle
boardPearl: Xi-Cleft point of the Yin Wei Mai. Important for acute mental/emotional disorders — mania, epilepsy, and psychiatric conditions. Also classically indicated for calming the fetus during pregnancy. Remember: KD-9 = Xi-Cleft of Yin Wei Mai.
memoryTrick: Guest House = the Yin Wei Mai takes shelter (as a guest) here. The guest house provides safety — used to calm the spirit in mental disorders and to calm the fetus. KD-9 = Yin Wei Xi-Cleft.

name: Yin Gu
location: On the medial side of the popliteal fossa, between the tendons of the semitendinosus and semimembranosus muscles, level with the popliteal crease
indications: Pain in the knee and popliteal fossa
boardPearl: He-Sea/Water point of the Kidney channel. Water within Water — the Horary point. Powerfully nourishes Kidney Yin. He-Sea points treat diseases of the fu organs and diarrhea. This point especially treats genito-urinary disorders: impotence, hernia, uterine bleeding, and urinary problems.
memoryTrick: Yin Valley = the valley (He-Sea) of Yin. Located in the Yin valley of the knee (popliteal fossa, medial side). Water element on the Water channel = double Yin/Water power. The deepest valley of Kidney Yin.
cautions: Avoid the popliteal artery and vein

name: Heng Gu
location: 5 cun below the umbilicus, on the superior border of the pubic symphysis, 0.5 cun lateral to the anterior midline (Ren-2)
categories: Meeting point of KD and Chong Mai
indications: Fullness and pain of the lower abdomen
boardPearl: Meeting point of the Kidney channel with the Chong Mai. KD-11 through KD-21 all meet with the Chong Mai. From KD-11 to KD-21, the Kidney channel runs 0.5 cun lateral to the Ren Mai on the abdomen. Treats genito-urinary disorders.
memoryTrick: Pubic Bone = located right at the pubic bone. The name tells you the location. First of the abdominal KD-Chong Mai meeting points (KD-11 to KD-21).
cautions: Full bladder should be emptied before needling
specialCategories: Meeting of KD and Chong Mai

name: Da He
location: 4 cun below the umbilicus, 0.5 cun lateral to the anterior midline (Ren-3 level)
categories: Meeting point of KD and Chong Mai
boardPearl: Meeting point of the Kidney channel with the Chong Mai. At the level of Ren-3 (Front-Mu of the Bladder). Treats reproductive and genital disorders. All abdominal Kidney points (KD-11 to KD-21) are 0.5 cun lateral to the midline.
memoryTrick: Great Manifestation = the great manifestation of Kidney Jing in the lower abdomen, near the reproductive organs. Located at the level of Ren-3.
specialCategories: Meeting of KD and Chong Mai

name: Qi Xue
location: 3 cun below the umbilicus, 0.5 cun lateral to the anterior midline (Ren-4 level)
categories: Meeting point of KD and Chong Mai
boardPearl: Meeting point of KD and Chong Mai at the level of Ren-4 (Front-Mu of the Small Intestine and Gate of Yuan Qi). This point accesses the Qi of both the Kidney and the Chong Mai. The Chong Mai is the Sea of Blood — used for menstrual disorders.
memoryTrick: Qi Hole = the hole where Kidney Qi and Chong Mai Qi meet. Located beside Ren-4, the origin of Yuan Qi. Think of a hole in the earth where Qi collects — right at the body's energetic center.
specialCategories: Meeting of KD and Chong Mai

name: Si Man
location: 2 cun below the umbilicus, 0.5 cun lateral to the anterior midline (Ren-5 level)
categories: Meeting point of KD and Chong Mai
boardPearl: Meeting point of KD and Chong Mai. The name Fourfold Fullness refers to the four types of abdominal masses or fullness this point can treat. Important for abdominal distension, masses, and menstrual disorders.
memoryTrick: Fourfold Fullness = treats the four kinds of fullness/masses in the lower abdomen. At the level of Ren-5, 2 cun below the umbilicus.
specialCategories: Meeting of KD and Chong Mai

name: Zhong Zhu
location: 1 cun below the umbilicus, 0.5 cun lateral to the anterior midline (Ren-7 level)
categories: Meeting point of KD and Chong Mai
boardPearl: Meeting point of KD and Chong Mai. The name Central Flow refers to the central flow of Qi through the Chong Mai. Not to be confused with SJ-3 Zhong Zhu (same pinyin, different characters and channel).
memoryTrick: Central Flow = Qi flows centrally through the Chong Mai at the center of the abdomen. Don't confuse with SJ-3 (also called Zhong Zhu but meaning 'Central Islet').
specialCategories: Meeting of KD and Chong Mai

name: Huang Shu
location: 0.5 cun lateral to the center of the umbilicus (Ren-8 level)
categories: Meeting point of KD and Chong Mai
boardPearl: Meeting point of KD and Chong Mai at the level of the umbilicus (Ren-8). 'Huang' refers to the vital membranes/Huang — the fatty tissue around the heart and below the diaphragm. This point treats abdominal disorders related to the Huang region. Important for intestinal disorders.
memoryTrick: Vitals Transporting Point = transports Qi to the vital Huang membranes. Located beside the navel (Ren-8). The Huang is the region of vital membranes — think of the vital center of the body.
specialCategories: Meeting of KD and Chong Mai

name: Shang Qu
location: 2 cun above the umbilicus, 0.5 cun lateral to the anterior midline (Ren-10 level)
categories: Meeting point of KD and Chong Mai
boardPearl: Meeting point of KD and Chong Mai. Located at the level of Ren-10, in the upper abdomen. Treats digestive disorders — the Kidney channel supports the Spleen and Stomach function in the middle jiao through its Chong Mai connection.
memoryTrick: Shang Bend = the channel bends at the Shang (upper region) of the abdomen. Now we are above the navel — moving into the middle jiao territory.
specialCategories: Meeting of KD and Chong Mai

name: Shi Guan
location: 3 cun above the umbilicus, 0.5 cun lateral to the anterior midline (Ren-11 level)
categories: Meeting point of KD and Chong Mai
boardPearl: Meeting point of KD and Chong Mai. 'Stone Pass' suggests obstruction — stone-like blockages in the abdomen. Used for infertility (the stone blocking conception), constipation, and abdominal masses. The stone must be passed for Qi to flow.
memoryTrick: Stone Pass = passing through stone-like obstructions. Think of breaking through blockages — infertility (stone womb), constipation (stone-like stool), and abdominal masses.
specialCategories: Meeting of KD and Chong Mai

name: Yin Du
location: 4 cun above the umbilicus, 0.5 cun lateral to the anterior midline (Ren-12 level)
categories: Meeting point of KD and Chong Mai
boardPearl: Meeting point of KD and Chong Mai at the level of Ren-12 (Front-Mu of Stomach and Hui-Meeting of Fu). The Yin Metropolis is a major hub — beside the most important abdominal point. Treats Stomach and digestive disorders via the KD-Chong Mai connection.
memoryTrick: Yin Metropolis = a great Yin city/hub beside the Stomach Mu point (Ren-12). A metropolis is a center of activity — this is where Kidney Yin meets the Stomach.
specialCategories: Meeting of KD and Chong Mai

name: Fu Tong Gu
location: 5 cun above the umbilicus, 0.5 cun lateral to the anterior midline (Ren-13 level)
categories: Meeting point of KD and Chong Mai
boardPearl: Meeting point of KD and Chong Mai. Located at the upper abdomen near the epigastrium. The last of the abdominal Chong Mai meeting points before the channel enters the chest. Treats upper digestive disorders.
memoryTrick: Abdomen Connecting Valley = the abdominal valley where the channel connects before ascending into the chest. Not to be confused with UB-66 Tong Gu.
specialCategories: Meeting of KD and Chong Mai

name: You Men
location: 6 cun above the umbilicus, 0.5 cun lateral to the anterior midline (Ren-14 level)
categories: Meeting point of KD and Chong Mai
boardPearl: Meeting point of KD and Chong Mai at the level of Ren-14 (Front-Mu of the Heart). The last of the Chong Mai meeting points. 'Dark Gate' = the gate between the abdomen and chest, the hidden passage. Transitional point — treats both epigastric and chest disorders.
memoryTrick: Dark Gate = the dark (hidden, Yin) gate between the abdomen and the chest. The channel passes through this gate to enter the thorax. Located beside the Heart Mu point (Ren-14).
cautions: Do not puncture deeply — risk of penetrating the peritoneum or liver
specialCategories: Meeting of KD and Chong Mai

name: Bu Lang
location: In the fifth intercostal space, 2 cun lateral to the anterior midline
indications: Distension of the chest and hypochondrium
boardPearl: First of the chest Kidney points. From KD-22 to KD-27, the channel widens to 2 cun lateral to the midline (compared to 0.5 cun on the abdomen). These chest points primarily treat cough, asthma, and chest disorders through the Kidney's role in grasping Lung Qi.
memoryTrick: Walking Corridor = the Kidney channel walks along a corridor (the chest). The corridor widens to 2 cun lateral — wider spacing than the abdominal points.
cautions: Do not puncture deeply — risk of pneumothorax

name: Shen Feng
location: In the fourth intercostal space, 2 cun lateral to the anterior midline
boardPearl: Chest Kidney point. 'Shen' (Spirit) in the name reflects the Kidney's role in housing the Zhi (Will) and its connection to the Heart's Shen. Located at the 4th intercostal space — close to the Heart. Treats chest and Lung disorders.
memoryTrick: Spirit Seal = the Kidney's spirit (Zhi/Will) is sealed in the chest near the Heart. Think of the Kidney Qi reaching up to support the Heart spirit.
cautions: Do not puncture deeply — risk of pneumothorax

name: Ling Xu
location: In the third intercostal space, 2 cun lateral to the anterior midline
boardPearl: Chest Kidney point in the 3rd intercostal space. 'Ling Xu' suggests a place where the spirit (Ling) resides in emptiness (Xu). Treats cough, asthma, and chest pain. These chest KD points are clinically used for Kidney not grasping Qi patterns.
memoryTrick: Spirit Burial Ground = the spirit's resting place. A quiet, empty space in the chest. Ascending one intercostal space at a time from KD-22 to KD-27.
cautions: Do not puncture deeply — risk of pneumothorax

name: Shen Cang
location: In the second intercostal space, 2 cun lateral to the anterior midline
boardPearl: Chest Kidney point in the 2nd intercostal space. 'Spirit Storehouse' = where the Kidney stores its spiritual aspect in the chest. An important point for severe asthma and breathlessness, especially when the Kidney fails to grasp Qi. The Shen (spirit/Kidney essence) is stored (Cang) here.
memoryTrick: Spirit Storehouse = the Kidney Shen is stored (Cang = storehouse) in the chest. Think of the Kidney's treasury in the upper body — where Kidney Qi supports the Lung and Heart.
cautions: Do not puncture deeply — risk of pneumothorax

name: Yu Zhong
location: In the first intercostal space, 2 cun lateral to the anterior midline
boardPearl: Chest Kidney point in the 1st intercostal space. The 'Lively Center' reflects the vitality of Kidney Qi rising to the chest. Penultimate point before KD-27. Treats chest disorders, especially related to Kidney Qi deficiency failing to support respiration.
memoryTrick: Lively Center = the center of liveliness where Kidney Qi animates the chest. One space below KD-27 (the final point under the clavicle).
cautions: Do not puncture deeply — risk of pneumothorax

name: Shu Fu
location: In the depression on the lower border of the clavicle, 2 cun lateral to the anterior midline
boardPearl: Final point of the Kidney channel — located just below the clavicle. 'Shu Mansion' = the mansion where all the Kidney Shu-transporting Qi arrives at its final destination. A very important clinical point for Kidney failing to grasp Lung Qi — treats chronic asthma and dyspnea. Often used with Ren-17 for descending rebellious Qi.
memoryTrick: Shu Mansion = the grand mansion (final destination) of the Kidney Shu-Qi. The Kidney channel's journey ends at this stately mansion under the clavicle. 'Fu' = mansion/palace — the endpoint where all Kidney Qi collects in the chest.
cautions: Do not puncture deeply — risk of pneumothorax

name: Tian Chi
location: 1 cun lateral to the nipple, in the fourth intercostal space
categories: Meeting point of PC, LV, and GB
indications: Swelling and pain of the axillary lymph nodes
boardPearl: Window of Heaven point — treats disorders of the head and regulates Qi ascending/descending. Meeting point of PC with Liver and Gallbladder channels. Located near the nipple, treats breast disorders. Windows of Heaven points are commonly tested — know them all.
memoryTrick: Celestial Pool = a heavenly pool (Window of Heaven) near the nipple. The pool of celestial Qi gathers in the chest. PC-1 = Window of Heaven — the only one on the PC channel.
cautions: Do not puncture deeply — risk of pneumothorax. Avoid the nipple.

name: Tian Quan
location: 2 cun below the anterior axillary fold, between the two heads of the biceps brachii muscle
indications: Distension of the chest and hypochondrium
Pain in the medial aspect of the upper arm
boardPearl: Located on the medial upper arm, 2 cun below the anterior axillary fold. 'Celestial Spring' = the spring of Qi from the heavenly (chest) region flowing down the arm. Treats cardiac and chest pain. Not frequently tested but location may appear on the exam.
memoryTrick: Celestial Spring = a heavenly spring that flows from the chest down the arm. Two 'Tian' (Celestial) points start the PC channel — PC-1 Celestial Pool, PC-2 Celestial Spring.
cautions: Avoid the brachial artery

name: Qu Ze
location: At the cubital crease, on the ulnar side of the tendon of the biceps brachii muscle
depth: 0.5-1 cun perpendicularly, or prick to bleed
indications: Tremor of the hand and arm
Pain of the elbow and arm
boardPearl: He-Sea/Water point and Sedation point of the Pericardium channel. Water is the child of Fire — sedates excess in the Pericardium/Heart. Bled to clear Heat from the blood (summer heat, febrile disease). He-Sea points treat stomach and intestinal disorders. Important for acute vomiting and diarrhea (especially with heat signs).
memoryTrick: Marsh at the Bend = a marsh (Water/He-Sea) at the bend of the elbow. Just like LU-5 'Cubit Marsh' — both are He-Sea/Water at the elbow crease. Water sedates Fire (child sedates mother).
cautions: Avoid the brachial artery; commonly pricked to bleed

name: Xi Men
location: 5 cun above the transverse crease of the wrist, on the line connecting PC-3 and PC-7, between the tendons of palmaris longus and flexor carpi radialis
boardPearl: Xi-Cleft point of the Pericardium channel. Xi-Cleft points treat acute conditions — for Yin channels, they especially treat bleeding. PC-4 is critical for acute cardiac pain, chest pain, and blood stasis in the chest. Key for acute heart conditions: angina, palpitations, and hematemesis. Think: heart attack emergency point.
memoryTrick: Xi-Cleft Gate = the gate where Qi and Blood accumulate deeply (Xi = cleft). The emergency gate for acute heart/chest pain and bleeding. If someone grabs their chest — think PC-4.

name: Jian Shi
