location: On the knee, in the medial depression (hollow) formed when the knee is flexed, in the depression medial to the patellar ligament
depth: Oblique insertion 0.5-1 cun towards the center of the knee joint, with the knee flexed
boardPearl: The MEDIAL eye of the knee — paired with ST-35 (Du Bi) which is the lateral eye. Boards test: ST-35 is the LATERAL knee eye (a regular channel point), while EX-LE-4 is the MEDIAL knee eye (an extra point). Together they form Xi Yan (EX-LE-5).
memoryTrick: NEI (inner/medial) XI (knee) YAN (eye) — the INNER EYE of the KNEE. The two dimples below the kneecap are the 'eyes' — this is the medial one. ST-35 (Du Bi) is its lateral partner.
cautions: Needle with the knee flexed. Avoid intra-articular infection — maintain strict sterile technique.

name: Xi Yan
location: On the knee, in the two depressions medial and lateral to the patellar ligament when the knee is flexed. The lateral point is identical to ST-35 (Du Bi); the medial point is EX-LE-4 (Nei Xi Yan).
depth: Oblique insertion 0.5-1 cun towards the center of the knee, with the knee flexed
indications: Weakness and motor impairment of the lower extremity
boardPearl: Xi Yan refers to BOTH knee eyes together. The lateral Xi Yan = ST-35 (Du Bi, a Stomach channel point). The medial Xi Yan = EX-LE-4 (Nei Xi Yan, an extra point). Board trick: when they ask about 'Xi Yan' as an extra point, they mean the pair; ST-35 alone is a channel point.
memoryTrick: XI (knee) YAN (eyes) — the two EYES of the KNEE. When you flex your knee, the kneecap looks like a face with two eyes (the hollows below it on each side of the patellar ligament).
cautions: Needle with the knee flexed. Maintain strict sterile technique to avoid joint infection.

name: Dan Nang Xue
location: On the right lower leg, at the tender point approximately 1-2 cun directly below GB-34 (Yang Ling Quan)
indications: Muscular atrophy of the lower limbs
boardPearl: THE empirical point for gallbladder disease. Located 1-2 cun below GB-34 — found by palpating for tenderness (Ah Shi-like). Boards test: Dan Nang Xue is the TENDER point below GB-34 used for cholecystitis and gallstones. Located on the RIGHT leg (gallbladder is on the right).
memoryTrick: DAN NANG (gallbladder) XUE (point) — literally GALLBLADDER POINT. It is 1-2 cun below GB-34 and found by pressing for tenderness. The gallbladder channel point (GB-34) is above; the gallbladder organ point is below.

name: Lan Wei Xue
location: On the right lower leg, approximately 2 cun below ST-36 (Zu San Li), at the tender point
boardPearl: THE empirical point for appendicitis. Located 2 cun below ST-36 — found by palpating for tenderness. Board parallel: Dan Nang Xue (EX-LE-6) is below GB-34 for gallbladder; Lan Wei Xue (EX-LE-7) is below ST-36 for appendix. Both are tender points on the RIGHT leg.
memoryTrick: LAN WEI (appendix) XUE (point) — APPENDIX POINT. Below ST-36 just as the appendix hangs below the cecum. Dan Nang = gallbladder below GB-34, Lan Wei = appendix below ST-36. Organ points below their channel points.
cautions: Appendicitis may require emergency surgical evaluation. Use acupuncture as adjunct, not sole treatment for acute appendicitis.

name: Nei Huai Jian
location: On the medial ankle, at the tip (most prominent point) of the medial malleolus
depth: Moxibustion is the primary method (3-7 cones); or prick to bleed
boardPearl: Located at the tip of the medial malleolus — primarily treated with MOXA. Board trick: an ankle point that treats TOOTHACHE (distal point treatment via channel connections). Paired with EX-LE-9 (Wai Huai Jian) at the lateral malleolus.
memoryTrick: NEI (medial) HUAI (malleolus) JIAN (tip) — the TIP of the INNER ankle bone. The pointy tip of the medial malleolus, mainly for moxa. Inner ankle tip for tooth pain — an unexpected connection.
cautions: Primarily moxa — needling is shallow or prick to bleed.

name: Wai Huai Jian
location: On the lateral ankle, at the tip (most prominent point) of the lateral malleolus
depth: Moxibustion is the primary method (3-7 cones); or prick to bleed
boardPearl: Located at the tip of the lateral malleolus — primarily MOXA like its medial partner EX-LE-8. Boards may test both malleolar tips together for toothache treatment via moxibustion.
memoryTrick: WAI (lateral/outer) HUAI (malleolus) JIAN (tip) — the TIP of the OUTER ankle bone. The lateral partner to EX-LE-8. Two ankle tip points, both mainly for moxa and toothache.
cautions: Primarily moxa — needling is shallow or prick to bleed.

name: Ba Feng
location: On the dorsum of both feet, at the webs between each pair of toes, at the junction of the red and white skin, 8 points in total (4 on each foot)
depth: Oblique insertion 0.5-0.8 cun proximally, or prick to bleed
indications: Pain and numbness of the toes
Redness and swelling of the dorsum of the foot
boardPearl: 8 points total (4 per foot) in the toe webs — the FOOT equivalent of Ba Xie (EX-UE-9) for the hands. Boards test: Ba Xie (Eight Evils) = hands, Ba Feng (Eight Winds) = feet. Both are 8 points in the web spaces, both can be bled.
memoryTrick: BA (eight) FENG (winds) — EIGHT WINDS blowing between the toes. XIE (evil) is in the hands, FENG (wind) is at the feet — wind blows at ground level, touching the feet first.

name: Du Yin
location: On the plantar surface of the 2nd toe, at the midpoint of the distal interphalangeal (DIP) joint transverse crease
boardPearl: Located on the PLANTAR surface of the 2nd toe at the DIP crease. Board classic for DIFFICULT LABOR (dystocia) and retained placenta. The 2nd toe connects to the Stomach channel — tested for its unique obstetric indication from such a distal point.
memoryTrick: DU YIN (Solitary Yin) — a SOLITARY lonely YIN point on the bottom of the 2nd toe. It helps the solitary mother in difficult labor. The only plantar toe point for obstetric emergencies.
cautions: Contraindicated during pregnancy unless labor is desired. Use for difficult labor only when appropriate.
specialCategories: Contraindicated in pregnancy unless for labor

name: Qi Duan
location: On the tips of all ten toes, approximately 0.1 cun distal to each toenail
depth: Prick to bleed (0.1-0.2 cun) with a three-edged needle, or moxibustion
indications: Numbness and pain of the toes
Redness and swelling of the dorsum of the foot
boardPearl: The foot equivalent of Shi Xuan (EX-UE-11). Located at the tips of all 10 toes. Boards test: Shi Xuan (Ten Dispersions) = fingertips for emergency/coma; Qi Duan (Qi Extremity) = toe tips for toe numbness and foot disorders. The foot version is less dramatic than the hand version.
memoryTrick: QI DUAN (Qi Extremity/Qi End) — the very END of qi at the tips of the toes, the most distal extremity of the body. The toe version of Shi Xuan. Qi reaches its end point (duan) at the tips of the toes.
